title: ranunculus compar
intro: Ranunculus compar, also known as lesser celandine or pilewort, is a perennial plant native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of ranunculin, a toxic compound that can be used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Ranunculus compar has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing swelling and pain, and has been used to treat conditions such as arthritis and gout. The plant can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or salve, and is often used in herbal medicine to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
